House of worship | Location={{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843}}{{ill|lt=Moka City|Q498060|WD=Q498060}}Togo 937 | Location={{Coord|36|26|57.1|N|140|01|33.2|E|scale:5000_region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Omae Shrine}} | Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi Okami}}
{{ill|lt=Kotoshirosu|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshirosu Okami}} | Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}} | Founding={{ill|lt=Jingo Keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}} years ({{ill|lt=767 years|Q30561|WD=Q30561}} - {{ill|lt=770 years|Q25021|WD=Q25021}}) | Main shrine={{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=November 9th|Q2991|WD=Q2991}}・{{ill|lt=November 10th|Q2988|WD=Q2988|lt=10th}} | Map={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Omae Shrine|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Osaki Shrine'' (Osaki Jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843}}{{ill|lt=Moka City|Q498060|WD=Q498060}}. {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha}}. {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}}. ===Enshrined God=== *God of fortune “Daikoku-sama” ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}) *His son god “Ebisu-sama” ({{ill|lt=Kotodaishu|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotodaishu Okami}}) *As the enshrined deity, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}}, {{ill|lt=Yoyorozu no Kami|Q118906758|WD=Q118906758}} It was called ``Omae-sama'' and ``Omae-daigongen'' by parishioners who worshiped it, and even today, the elders are familiar with it as ``{{ill|lt=Gongen|Q3080343|WD=Q3080343}}-sama,'' and most people call it ``Omae-sama.'' ===History=== Osaki, the sacred place where Engishikinai Omae Shrine is located, is said to be a ``sacred place from the age of the gods'' in ancient oral inscriptions. The origin of the name ``Omae'' comes from the fact that ``Haganuma'' once existed in the northern area adjacent to the shrine precincts, and because it was located at the tip of the lake, it was called ``Omae (Saki) Shrine.'' The shrine building was rebuilt during {{ill|lt=Jingo Keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}} ({{ill|lt=767|Q30561|WD=Q30561}} - {{ill|lt=770|Q25021|WD=Q25021}}), and has a history of over 1,500 years. During the Heian period, the branch temple of Higashieizan Kuroko Senmyo-ji Temple in Bando, Hitachi Province was enshrined as a Bettoji temple, and the head office had a {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|WD=Q854773}} as a syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism. Also, {{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Johei}}5 years ({{ill|lt=935 years|Q24867|WD=Q24867}}) {{ill|lt=Taira no Masakado|Q1207652|WD=Q1207652}} is {{ill|lt=Joheiten It is said that when he started the Kei no War|Q2975322|WD=Q2975322}}, he prayed for victory in the battle at Omae Shrine and performed a service at the Mitarai of {{ill|lt=Gogyo River|Q11372661|WD=Q11372661}}. In the Middle Ages, the {{ill|lt=Haga clan|Q11615839|WD=Q11615839}} of Bando's rough warriors ``Ki-Kei Ryōtō'' built Wakairo Castle to the east of the headquarters, then {{ill|lt=Gozen Castle|Q24872500|WD=Q24872500}} to the south of Omae Shrine, and served as the guardian of the Omae Shrine shrine territory until the 22nd generation, Haga Juro Kiyohara Takasada.It is said that in the past, during {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Reitaisai|1=UNKNOWN}}, people chanted ``to'' and offered the head of a boar or deer, and it is said that deer meat and deer heads were offered as offerings to the gods. Even now, venison is offered as a sacred food at annual festivals. Additionally, a carp representing a three-tailed divine messenger is depicted on the large floor ceiling of the worship hall, and an image of a rising dragon is depicted on the ceiling of the worship hall. The main hall is also carved with male and female carp and Kotone Sennin's carp. In honor of the carp that is the {{ill|lt=divine messenger|Q10444973|WD=Q10444973}}, it is said that when a wish is fulfilled, a healthy carp is offered in front of the god, and the ritual is performed by pouring sacred sake over it and releasing it into the {{ill|lt=Gogyo River|Q11372661|WD=Q11372661}}. In addition, ``Japan's No. 1 Ebisu Shrine {{ill|lt=Omae Ebisu Shrine|Q11433058|WD=Q11433058}}'' is enshrined on the grounds. [[File:Ashioyama Shrine.jpg|alt=Ashioyama Shrine|thumb|230x230px|Ashioyama Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bike Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}})]]{{ill|lt=2013|Q1998|WD=Q1998}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}25) On November 9th, the second torii gate, which had been forced to be dismantled due to the Great East Japan Earthquake, was rebuilt using cedar wood from within the prefecture. Also, in commemoration of the 62nd Jingu Shikinen Sengu, maintenance work was carried out on the {{ill|lt=Setsusha Shrine|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=Setsusha/Sususha}}, which is enshrined behind the main shrine. Among them, Ashioyama Shrine, the last of its kind, is the first shrine in Japan to pray for traffic safety for {{ill|lt=motorcycles|Q34493|WD=Q34493|lt=two-wheeled vehicles}}, and is visited by 800 to 1,000 riders a month from inside and outside the prefecture every year. [https://bike-news.jp/post/171636 2020.05.28 Bike News Editorial Department What is there at Bike Shrine? "Omae Shrine" in Tochigi Prefecture is a pilgrimage spot that also appears in the manga "Bakuon!!" Motorcycle News] {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|WD=Q25290}} (Heisei 217) marks the 1250th anniversary of the restoration, and we are promoting commemorative projects such as relocation and renovation of the temple hall, kagura hall, shrine office, etc. From the Great Setsubun Festival on February 3 of the same year, {{ill|lt=Oiin|Q5371419|WD=Q5371419|lt=Oiin ceremony}}, the three demons ({{ill|lt=Mae-oni, Go-oni|Q11397864|WD=Q11397864}} In addition to {{ill|lt=Domoku|Q60995805|WD=Q60995805}}), {{ill|lt=Oiin|Q5371419|WD=Q5371419|lt=Hosoji}} was added in accordance with the ancient rituals.{{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291}} As of December 25, 2018, Omae Shrine's main hall, shrine, and worship hall were designated as national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Properties|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}Heisei 30 December 25th, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology No. 231[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1410231.html “About the designation of national treasures and important cultural properties (buildings)”] (Agency for Cultural Affairs website, announced October 19, 2018). File:Oosaki-keidai.jpg|Omae Shrine precincts File:Oosakijinjya-kaguraden.jpg |Kaguraden File:Oosakiebisusama.jpg|Omaebisu Shrine ===Festival=== *Saidan Festival/New Year Special Prayer Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1st|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) *Ebisu Kosai Festival ({{ill|lt=January 10th|Q2243|WD=Q2243}}・December 10th) *Setsubun Festival/Oishi Ceremony ({{ill|lt=February 3rd|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}) *National Foundation Day Celebration ({{ill|lt=February 11th|Q2335|WD=Q2335}}) * Prayer Festival ({{ill|lt=March 28th|Q2458|WD=Q2458}}) {{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260}} dedication *Flower offering festival (flower arrangement head Ikenobo Utsunomiya branch) ({{ill|lt=May 1st|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}) *Chinka Festival/Children's Ikebana Experience Class ({{ill|lt=May 3rd|Q2547|WD=Q2547}}) *Summer festival/Chino ring ritual ({{ill|lt=July 31st|Q2715|WD=Q2715}}) *Shichi-Go-San prayer (during November) *Tanabata Festival (August 7th) *Regular festival (November 9th and 10th) Kagura dedication *Ninamesai ({{ill|lt=November 27th|Q3018|WD=Q3018}}) Kagura dedication *Prayer festival for eliminating fire and theft ({{ill|lt=December 1st|Q2297|WD=Q2297}}) *Tencho Festival ({{ill|lt=December 23rd|Q2676|WD=Q2676}}) *New Year's Eve/Great Purification Ceremony ({{ill|lt=December 31st|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) ===Sessuesha=== Setsusha (outside shrine) *Kousei Shrine *Atago Shrine Shusha (precinct shrine) *Kodai Shrine/Omononushi Okunitama Shrine *Ara Shrine *(Ninomiya) Inari Shrine *Inari Shrine *Rain Shrine *Koyasu Shrine *Tenmangu Shrine *Kotohira Shrine *Awashima Shrine *Ashioyama Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bike Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}) *Ninomiya Shrine *Osugi Shrine File:Oosaki-yasakajinjya.jpg|Yasaka Shrine File:Oosaki-oomononusijinjya.jpg|Kodai Shrine/Omononushi Okunitama Shrine File:Oosaki-tenmanguu.jpg|Tenmangu Shrine ===Cultural assets=== ===Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated)=== *Honden - Built in the middle of the Edo period (1707). The building has three girder rows, two beam spaces, a hip-gable structure, one room facing the roof, and a copper plate roof. Designated on December 25, 2018. *Hideden and Worship Hall - Built in the middle of the Edo period. Hall of worship, 3 rooms in the front with girders, 5 rooms in the back, 3 rooms between the beams, gambrel style, front with staggered gables, 1 room on the front, with eaves and gables, copper plate roofing. The hall has two girder rows, one beam space, hipped structure, and copper plate roofing. Designated on December 25, 2018. ===Tochigi Prefecture designated cultural property=== *{{ill|lt=Ryobe Torii|Q11362685|WD=Q11362685}} *One copper lantern *Tachi Mumei Kuchichi *Paperback and ink calligraphy Heike Monogatari 11 volumes *Version of the Great Prajnaparamaya Sutra, Volume 586 *Genroku picture map *Yamauchi Meifu Merit Monument *Sangaku *Eitai Odaimikagura Monument *Omae Shrine Odai Kagura === Transportation === * Nearest station: 15 minutes walk from Moka Railway "{{ill|lt=Kita-Moka Station|Q6417569|WD=Q6417569}}" * {{ill|lt=Kitakanto Expressway|Q11404370|WD=Q11404370}} About 10 minutes from Moka I.C. * From JR {{ill|lt=Ishibashi Station (Tochigi Prefecture)|Q872787|WD=Q872787|lt=Ishibashi Station}}, take the {{ill|lt=Kanto Expressway (Tochigi Prefecture)|Q11655986|WD=Q11655986|lt=Kanto Bus}} "Ishibashi Station/Moka Shako" system for about 35 minutes, get off at "Moka Shako" and walk 20 minutes. * From JR {{ill|lt=Utsunomiya Station|Q801549|WD=Q801549}}, take the Kanto bus bound for “Utsunomiya Tobu-Moka Office” for about an hour, and get off at “Omae Jinja-mae”. * Parking: Yes === Collaboration === ; Sora-chan (Sora-chan) : The main character of "Sora-chan's Journey", which is serialized in the reader submission section of the magazine "Mr.Bike BG" of {{ill|lt=Motor Magazine Company|Q11344833|WD=Q11344833}} by the manga contributor CB of Red Feathers{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/masiko-kyokusin/entry-11829355564.html |title=Sora-chan's Journey |date=2014-04-21 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Red Feather CB Blog}}. : Poster because Ashioyama Shrine, a subsidiary shrine, is called a motorcycle shrine |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto faith~}}Life-size figures{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/oosakijinja/entry-11422708297.html |title=Riders from all over the country! ! |date=2012-12-08 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto Faith~}} is located, and we are collaborating.: {{ill|lt=2013|Q1998|WD=Q1998}} Since January 27, 2013, an event called "Sora-chan Sanshu" (only the first "Sora-chan Meeting") has been held regularly. Plenty of photos |date=2013-01-27 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto faith~}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/oosakijinja/entry-12423290082.html |title=Information about the 7th Sora-chan gathering! |date=2018-12-03 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto Faith~}}.: In addition, in conjunction with "Sora-chan Collection", illustrator Watabe☆Yu has created {{ill|lt=Honda Motors|Q9584|WD=Q9584|lt=Honda}}, {{ill|lt=Kawasaki Heavy Industries|Q748148|WD=Q748148|lt=Kawasaki}}, {{jalink|Suzuki} (Companies)|Q181642|Suzuki}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaha Motor|Q158888|WD=Q158888|lt=Yamaha}} motorcycle ema are dedicated and regularly distributed{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/oosakijinja/entry-12382683852.html |title=Bike ema　Motorcycle ema from 4 domestic companies will be available soon! ! |date=2018-06-10 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto Faith~}}{{Cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190125183521/https://www.shimotsuke.co.jp/articles/-/45756 |title=Moka Omae Shrine with 4 domestic companies offering motorcycle votive tablets |date=2018-07-06 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |newspaper={{ill|lt=Shimotsu Shimbun|Q11361390|WD=Q11361390}}}}. ; {{ill|lt=Tsuina-chan|Q85869165|WD=Q85869165}}: Religious research related to the {{ill|lt=Japanese Oniko (character)|Q5365273|WD=Q5365273|lt=Japanese Oniko}} project that was born on the Internet bulletin board.{{ill|lt=Moe|Q497942|WD=Q497942}}{{ill|lt=Character|Q95074|WD=Q95074}}. He is said to be {{ill|lt=Oiin #Overview|Q5371419|WD=Q5371419|lt=Hosoji}}, a descendant of {{ill|lt=Yakusōkaku|Q198946|WD=Q198946}}.: Exchanges with Omae Shrine occurred due to the appearance of the Hoso clan in the Oinashiki ceremony in 2017 (Heisei 29). |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto Faith~}}, On Setsubun in {{ill|lt=2019|Q25274|WD=Q25274}} (Heisei 31), a goshuin stamp made with funds raised through crowdfunding was dedicated. As a result, goshuin stamps of Omae Shrine and Ashioyama Shrine (Bike Shrine) within the precincts were created and awarded for one month in February 2019. |title=[Kurafan] Let's make Setsubun Goshuin for Tsuina-chan and dedicate it to Omae Shrine❣️[Plan] |date=2018-10-24 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |work=Fantia [Onikko Hunter Tsuina-chan]}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/oosakijinja/entry-12414527938.html |title=Tsuina-chan goshuin dedication project |date=2018-10-26 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto faith~}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/oosakijinja/entry-12435182006.html |title=Current situation of cold red plum blossoms and Setsubun special goshuin |date=2019-01-24 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto faith~}}.: In 2019 (Reiwa 1), a story set at Omae Shrine was created in the voice drama "Onikko Hunter Tsuina-chan". ! {{ill|lt=Kotoshirosu|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshirosu Okami}} | Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}} | Founding={{ill|lt=Jingo Keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}} years ({{ill|lt=767 years|Q30561|WD=Q30561}} - {{ill|lt=770 years|Q25021|WD=Q25021}}) | Main shrine={{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=November 9th|Q2991|WD=Q2991}}・{{ill|lt=November 10th|Q2988|WD=Q2988|lt=10th}} | Map={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Omae Shrine|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Osaki Shrine'' (Osaki Jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843}}{{ill|lt=Moka City|Q498060|WD=Q498060}}. {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha}}. {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}}. ===Enshrined God=== *God of fortune “Daikoku-sama” ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}) *His son god “Ebisu-sama” ({{ill|lt=Kotodaishu|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotodaishu Okami}}) *As the enshrined deity, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}}, {{ill|lt=Yoyorozu no Kami|Q118906758|WD=Q118906758}} It was called ``Omae-sama'' and ``Omae-daigongen'' by parishioners who worshiped it, and even today, the elders are familiar with it as ``{{ill|lt=Gongen|Q3080343|WD=Q3080343}}-sama,'' and most people call it ``Omae-sama.'' ===History=== Osaki, the sacred place where Engishikinai Omae Shrine is located, is said to be a ``sacred place from the age of the gods'' in ancient oral inscriptions. The origin of the name ``Omae'' comes from the fact that ``Haganuma'' once existed in the northern area adjacent to the shrine precincts, and because it was located at the tip of the lake, it was called ``Omae (Saki) Shrine.'' The shrine building was rebuilt during {{ill|lt=Jingo Keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}} ({{ill|lt=767|Q30561|WD=Q30561}} - {{ill|lt=770|Q25021|WD=Q25021}}), and has a history of over 1,500 years. During the Heian period, the branch temple of Higashieizan Kuroko Senmyo-ji Temple in Bando, Hitachi Province was enshrined as a Bettoji temple, and the head office had a {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|WD=Q854773}} as a syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism. Also, {{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Johei}}5 years ({{ill|lt=935 years|Q24867|WD=Q24867}}) {{ill|lt=Taira no Masakado|Q1207652|WD=Q1207652}} is {{ill|lt=Joheiten It is said that when he started the Kei no War|Q2975322|WD=Q2975322}}, he prayed for victory in the battle at Omae Shrine and performed a service at the Mitarai of {{ill|lt=Gogyo River|Q11372661|WD=Q11372661}}. In the Middle Ages, the {{ill|lt=Haga clan|Q11615839|WD=Q11615839}} of Bando's rough warriors ``Ki-Kei Ryōtō'' built Wakairo Castle to the east of the headquarters, then {{ill|lt=Gozen Castle|Q24872500|WD=Q24872500}} to the south of Omae Shrine, and served as the guardian of the Omae Shrine shrine territory until the 22nd generation, Haga Juro Kiyohara Takasada.It is said that in the past, during {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Reitaisai|1=UNKNOWN}}, people chanted ``to'' and offered the head of a boar or deer, and it is said that deer meat and deer heads were offered as offerings to the gods. Even now, venison is offered as a sacred food at annual festivals. Additionally, a carp representing a three-tailed divine messenger is depicted on the large floor ceiling of the worship hall, and an image of a rising dragon is depicted on the ceiling of the worship hall. The main hall is also carved with male and female carp and Kotone Sennin's carp. In honor of the carp that is the {{ill|lt=divine messenger|Q10444973|WD=Q10444973}}, it is said that when a wish is fulfilled, a healthy carp is offered in front of the god, and the ritual is performed by pouring sacred sake over it and releasing it into the {{ill|lt=Gogyo River|Q11372661|WD=Q11372661}}. In addition, ``Japan's No. 1 Ebisu Shrine {{ill|lt=Omae Ebisu Shrine|Q11433058|WD=Q11433058}}'' is enshrined on the grounds. [[File:Ashioyama Shrine.jpg|alt=Ashioyama Shrine|thumb|230x230px|Ashioyama Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bike Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}})]]{{ill|lt=2013|Q1998|WD=Q1998}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}25) On November 9th, the second torii gate, which had been forced to be dismantled due to the Great East Japan Earthquake, was rebuilt using cedar wood from within the prefecture. Also, in commemoration of the 62nd Jingu Shikinen Sengu, maintenance work was carried out on the {{ill|lt=Setsusha Shrine|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=Setsusha/Sususha}}, which is enshrined behind the main shrine. Among them, Ashioyama Shrine, the last of its kind, is the first shrine in Japan to pray for traffic safety for {{ill|lt=motorcycles|Q34493|WD=Q34493|lt=two-wheeled vehicles}}, and is visited by 800 to 1,000 riders a month from inside and outside the prefecture every year. [https://bike-news.jp/post/171636 2020.05.28 Bike News Editorial Department What is there at Bike Shrine? "Omae Shrine" in Tochigi Prefecture is a pilgrimage spot that also appears in the manga "Bakuon!!" Motorcycle News] {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|WD=Q25290}} (Heisei 217) marks the 1250th anniversary of the restoration, and we are promoting commemorative projects such as relocation and renovation of the temple hall, kagura hall, shrine office, etc. From the Great Setsubun Festival on February 3 of the same year, {{ill|lt=Oiin|Q5371419|WD=Q5371419|lt=Oiin ceremony}}, the three demons ({{ill|lt=Mae-oni, Go-oni|Q11397864|WD=Q11397864}} In addition to {{ill|lt=Domoku|Q60995805|WD=Q60995805}}), {{ill|lt=Oiin|Q5371419|WD=Q5371419|lt=Hosoji}} was added in accordance with the ancient rituals.{{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291}} As of December 25, 2018, Omae Shrine's main hall, shrine, and worship hall were designated as national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Properties|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}Heisei 30 December 25th, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology No. 231[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1410231.html “About the designation of national treasures and important cultural properties (buildings)”] (Agency for Cultural Affairs website, announced October 19, 2018). The building has three girder rows, two beam spaces, a hip-gable structure, one room facing the roof, and a copper plate roof. Designated on December 25, 2018. *Hideden and Worship Hall - Built in the middle of the Edo period. Hall of worship, 3 rooms in the front with girders, 5 rooms in the back, 3 rooms between the beams, gambrel style, front with staggered gables, 1 room on the front, with eaves and gables, copper plate roofing. The hall has two girder rows, one beam space, hipped structure, and copper plate roofing. Designated on December 25, 2018. ===Tochigi Prefecture designated cultural property=== *{{ill|lt=Ryobe Torii|Q11362685|WD=Q11362685}} *One copper lantern *Tachi Mumei Kuchichi *Paperback and ink calligraphy Heike Monogatari 11 volumes *Version of the Great Prajnaparamaya Sutra, Volume 586 *Genroku picture map *Yamauchi Meifu Merit Monument *Sangaku *Eitai Odaimikagura Monument *Omae Shrine Odai Kagura === Transportation === * Nearest station: 15 minutes walk from Moka Railway "{{ill|lt=Kita-Moka Station|Q6417569|WD=Q6417569}}" * {{ill|lt=Kitakanto Expressway|Q11404370|WD=Q11404370}} About 10 minutes from Moka I.C. * From JR {{ill|lt=Ishibashi Station (Tochigi Prefecture)|Q872787|WD=Q872787|lt=Ishibashi Station}}, take the {{ill|lt=Kanto Expressway (Tochigi Prefecture)|Q11655986|WD=Q11655986|lt=Kanto Bus}} "Ishibashi Station/Moka Shako" system for about 35 minutes, get off at "Moka Shako" and walk 20 minutes. * From JR {{ill|lt=Utsunomiya Station|Q801549|WD=Q801549}}, take the Kanto bus bound for “Utsunomiya Tobu-Moka Office” for about an hour, and get off at “Omae Jinja-mae”. * Parking: Yes === Collaboration === ; Sora-chan (Sora-chan) : The main character of "Sora-chan's Journey", which is serialized in the reader submission section of the magazine "Mr.Bike BG" of {{ill|lt=Motor Magazine Company|Q11344833|WD=Q11344833}} by the manga contributor CB of Red Feathers{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/masiko-kyokusin/entry-11829355564.html |title=Sora-chan's Journey |date=2014-04-21 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Red Feather CB Blog}}. : Poster because Ashioyama Shrine, a subsidiary shrine, is called a motorcycle shrine |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto faith~}}Life-size figures{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/oosakijinja/entry-11422708297.html |title=Riders from all over the country! Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}} | Founding={{ill|lt=Jingo Keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}} years ({{ill|lt=767 years|Q30561|WD=Q30561}} - {{ill|lt=770 years|Q25021|WD=Q25021}}) | Main shrine={{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=November 9th|Q2991|WD=Q2991}}・{{ill|lt=November 10th|Q2988|WD=Q2988|lt=10th}} | Map={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Omae Shrine|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Osaki Shrine'' (Osaki Jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843}}{{ill|lt=Moka City|Q498060|WD=Q498060}}. {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha}}. {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}}. ===Enshrined God=== *God of fortune “Daikoku-sama” ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}) *His son god “Ebisu-sama” ({{ill|lt=Kotodaishu|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotodaishu Okami}}) *As the enshrined deity, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}}, {{ill|lt=Yoyorozu no Kami|Q118906758|WD=Q118906758}} It was called ``Omae-sama'' and ``Omae-daigongen'' by parishioners who worshiped it, and even today, the elders are familiar with it as ``{{ill|lt=Gongen|Q3080343|WD=Q3080343}}-sama,'' and most people call it ``Omae-sama.'' ===History=== Osaki, the sacred place where Engishikinai Omae Shrine is located, is said to be a ``sacred place from the age of the gods'' in ancient oral inscriptions. The origin of the name ``Omae'' comes from the fact that ``Haganuma'' once existed in the northern area adjacent to the shrine precincts, and because it was located at the tip of the lake, it was called ``Omae (Saki) Shrine.'' The shrine building was rebuilt during {{ill|lt=Jingo Keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}} ({{ill|lt=767|Q30561|WD=Q30561}} - {{ill|lt=770|Q25021|WD=Q25021}}), and has a history of over 1,500 years. During the Heian period, the branch temple of Higashieizan Kuroko Senmyo-ji Temple in Bando, Hitachi Province was enshrined as a Bettoji temple, and the head office had a {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|WD=Q854773}} as a syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism. Also, {{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Johei}}5 years ({{ill|lt=935 years|Q24867|WD=Q24867}}) {{ill|lt=Taira no Masakado|Q1207652|WD=Q1207652}} is {{ill|lt=Joheiten It is said that when he started the Kei no War|Q2975322|WD=Q2975322}}, he prayed for victory in the battle at Omae Shrine and performed a service at the Mitarai of {{ill|lt=Gogyo River|Q11372661|WD=Q11372661}}. In the Middle Ages, the {{ill|lt=Haga clan|Q11615839|WD=Q11615839}} of Bando's rough warriors ``Ki-Kei Ryōtō'' built Wakairo Castle to the east of the headquarters, then {{ill|lt=Gozen Castle|Q24872500|WD=Q24872500}} to the south of Omae Shrine, and served as the guardian of the Omae Shrine shrine territory until the 22nd generation, Haga Juro Kiyohara Takasada.It is said that in the past, during {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Reitaisai|1=UNKNOWN}}, people chanted ``to'' and offered the head of a boar or deer, and it is said that deer meat and deer heads were offered as offerings to the gods. Even now, venison is offered as a sacred food at annual festivals. Additionally, a carp representing a three-tailed divine messenger is depicted on the large floor ceiling of the worship hall, and an image of a rising dragon is depicted on the ceiling of the worship hall. The main hall is also carved with male and female carp and Kotone Sennin's carp. In honor of the carp that is the {{ill|lt=divine messenger|Q10444973|WD=Q10444973}}, it is said that when a wish is fulfilled, a healthy carp is offered in front of the god, and the ritual is performed by pouring sacred sake over it and releasing it into the {{ill|lt=Gogyo River|Q11372661|WD=Q11372661}}. In addition, ``Japan's No. 1 Ebisu Shrine {{ill|lt=Omae Ebisu Shrine|Q11433058|WD=Q11433058}}'' is enshrined on the grounds. [[File:Ashioyama Shrine.jpg|alt=Ashioyama Shrine|thumb|230x230px|Ashioyama Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bike Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}})]]{{ill|lt=2013|Q1998|WD=Q1998}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}25) On November 9th, the second torii gate, which had been forced to be dismantled due to the Great East Japan Earthquake, was rebuilt using cedar wood from within the prefecture. Also, in commemoration of the 62nd Jingu Shikinen Sengu, maintenance work was carried out on the {{ill|lt=Setsusha Shrine|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=Setsusha/Sususha}}, which is enshrined behind the main shrine. Among them, Ashioyama Shrine, the last of its kind, is the first shrine in Japan to pray for traffic safety for {{ill|lt=motorcycles|Q34493|WD=Q34493|lt=two-wheeled vehicles}}, and is visited by 800 to 1,000 riders a month from inside and outside the prefecture every year. [https://bike-news.jp/post/171636 2020.05.28 Bike News Editorial Department What is there at Bike Shrine? "Omae Shrine" in Tochigi Prefecture is a pilgrimage spot that also appears in the manga "Bakuon!!" Motorcycle News] {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|WD=Q25290}} (Heisei 217) marks the 1250th anniversary of the restoration, and we are promoting commemorative projects such as relocation and renovation of the temple hall, kagura hall, shrine office, etc. From the Great Setsubun Festival on February 3 of the same year, {{ill|lt=Oiin|Q5371419|WD=Q5371419|lt=Oiin ceremony}}, the three demons ({{ill|lt=Mae-oni, Go-oni|Q11397864|WD=Q11397864}} In addition to {{ill|lt=Domoku|Q60995805|WD=Q60995805}}), {{ill|lt=Oiin|Q5371419|WD=Q5371419|lt=Hosoji}} was added in accordance with the ancient rituals.{{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291}} As of December 25, 2018, Omae Shrine's main hall, shrine, and worship hall were designated as national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Properties|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}Heisei 30 December 25th, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology No. 231[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1410231.html “About the designation of national treasures and important cultural properties (buildings)”] (Agency for Cultural Affairs website, announced October 19, 2018). File:Oosaki-keidai.jpg|Omae Shrine precincts File:Oosakijinjya-kaguraden.jpg |Kaguraden File:Oosakiebisusama.jpg|Omaebisu Shrine ===Festival=== *Saidan Festival/New Year Special Prayer Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1st|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) *Ebisu Kosai Festival ({{ill|lt=January 10th|Q2243|WD=Q2243}}・December 10th) *Setsubun Festival/Oishi Ceremony ({{ill|lt=February 3rd|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}) *National Foundation Day Celebration ({{ill|lt=February 11th|Q2335|WD=Q2335}}) * Prayer Festival ({{ill|lt=March 28th|Q2458|WD=Q2458}}) {{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260}} dedication *Flower offering festival (flower arrangement head Ikenobo Utsunomiya branch) ({{ill|lt=May 1st|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}) *Chinka Festival/Children's Ikebana Experience Class ({{ill|lt=May 3rd|Q2547|WD=Q2547}}) *Summer festival/Chino ring ritual ({{ill|lt=July 31st|Q2715|WD=Q2715}}) *Shichi-Go-San prayer (during November) *Tanabata Festival (August 7th) *Regular festival (November 9th and 10th) Kagura dedication *Ninamesai ({{ill|lt=November 27th|Q3018|WD=Q3018}}) Kagura dedication *Prayer festival for eliminating fire and theft ({{ill|lt=December 1st|Q2297|WD=Q2297}}) *Tencho Festival ({{ill|lt=December 23rd|Q2676|WD=Q2676}}) *New Year's Eve/Great Purification Ceremony ({{ill|lt=December 31st|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) ===Sessuesha=== Setsusha (outside shrine) *Kousei Shrine *Atago Shrine Shusha (precinct shrine) *Kodai Shrine/Omononushi Okunitama Shrine *Ara Shrine *(Ninomiya) Inari Shrine *Inari Shrine *Rain Shrine *Koyasu Shrine *Tenmangu Shrine *Kotohira Shrine *Awashima Shrine *Ashioyama Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bike Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}) *Ninomiya Shrine *Osugi Shrine File:Oosaki-yasakajinjya.jpg|Yasaka Shrine File:Oosaki-oomononusijinjya.jpg|Kodai Shrine/Omononushi Okunitama Shrine File:Oosaki-tenmanguu.jpg|Tenmangu Shrine ===Cultural assets=== ===Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated)=== *Honden - Built in the middle of the Edo period (1707). The building has three girder rows, two beam spaces, a hip-gable structure, one room facing the roof, and a copper plate roof. Designated on December 25, 2018. *Hideden and Worship Hall - Built in the middle of the Edo period. Hall of worship, 3 rooms in the front with girders, 5 rooms in the back, 3 rooms between the beams, gambrel style, front with staggered gables, 1 room on the front, with eaves and gables, copper plate roofing. The hall has two girder rows, one beam space, hipped structure, and copper plate roofing. Designated on December 25, 2018. ===Tochigi Prefecture designated cultural property=== *{{ill|lt=Ryobe Torii|Q11362685|WD=Q11362685}} *One copper lantern *Tachi Mumei Kuchichi *Paperback and ink calligraphy Heike Monogatari 11 volumes *Version of the Great Prajnaparamaya Sutra, Volume 586 *Genroku picture map *Yamauchi Meifu Merit Monument *Sangaku *Eitai Odaimikagura Monument *Omae Shrine Odai Kagura === Transportation === * Nearest station: 15 minutes walk from Moka Railway "{{ill|lt=Kita-Moka Station|Q6417569|WD=Q6417569}}" * {{ill|lt=Kitakanto Expressway|Q11404370|WD=Q11404370}} About 10 minutes from Moka I.C. * From JR {{ill|lt=Ishibashi Station (Tochigi Prefecture)|Q872787|WD=Q872787|lt=Ishibashi Station}}, take the {{ill|lt=Kanto Expressway (Tochigi Prefecture)|Q11655986|WD=Q11655986|lt=Kanto Bus}} "Ishibashi Station/Moka Shako" system for about 35 minutes, get off at "Moka Shako" and walk 20 minutes. * From JR {{ill|lt=Utsunomiya Station|Q801549|WD=Q801549}}, take the Kanto bus bound for “Utsunomiya Tobu-Moka Office” for about an hour, and get off at “Omae Jinja-mae”. * Parking: Yes === Collaboration === ; Sora-chan (Sora-chan) : The main character of "Sora-chan's Journey", which is serialized in the reader submission section of the magazine "Mr.Bike BG" of {{ill|lt=Motor Magazine Company|Q11344833|WD=Q11344833}} by the manga contributor CB of Red Feathers{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/masiko-kyokusin/entry-11829355564.html |title=Sora-chan's Journey |date=2014-04-21 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Red Feather CB Blog}}. : Poster because Ashioyama Shrine, a subsidiary shrine, is called a motorcycle shrine |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto faith~}}Life-size figures{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/oosakijinja/entry-11422708297.html |title=Riders from all over the country! ! |date=2012-12-08 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto Faith~}} is located, and we are collaborating.: {{ill|lt=2013|Q1998|WD=Q1998}} Since January 27, 2013, an event called "Sora-chan Sanshu" (only the first "Sora-chan Meeting") has been held regularly. Plenty of photos |date=2013-01-27 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto faith~}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/oosakijinja/entry-12423290082.html |title=Information about the 7th Sora-chan gathering! |date=2018-12-03 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto Faith~}}.: In addition, in conjunction with "Sora-chan Collection", illustrator Watabe☆Yu has created {{ill|lt=Honda Motors|Q9584|WD=Q9584|lt=Honda}}, {{ill|lt=Kawasaki Heavy Industries|Q748148|WD=Q748148|lt=Kawasaki}}, {{jalink|Suzuki} (Companies)|Q181642|Suzuki}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaha Motor|Q158888|WD=Q158888|lt=Yamaha}} motorcycle ema are dedicated and regularly distributed{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/oosakijinja/entry-12382683852.html |title=Bike ema　Motorcycle ema from 4 domestic companies will be available soon! ! |date=2018-06-10 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto Faith~}}{{Cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190125183521/https://www.shimotsuke.co.jp/articles/-/45756 |title=Moka Omae Shrine with 4 domestic companies offering motorcycle votive tablets |date=2018-07-06 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |newspaper={{ill|lt=Shimotsu Shimbun|Q11361390|WD=Q11361390}}}}. ; {{ill|lt=Tsuina-chan|Q85869165|WD=Q85869165}}: Religious research related to the {{ill|lt=Japanese Oniko (character)|Q5365273|WD=Q5365273|lt=Japanese Oniko}} project that was born on the Internet bulletin board.{{ill|lt=Moe|Q497942|WD=Q497942}}{{ill|lt=Character|Q95074|WD=Q95074}}. He is said to be {{ill|lt=Oiin #Overview|Q5371419|WD=Q5371419|lt=Hosoji}}, a descendant of {{ill|lt=Yakusōkaku|Q198946|WD=Q198946}}.: Exchanges with Omae Shrine occurred due to the appearance of the Hoso clan in the Oinashiki ceremony in 2017 (Heisei 29). |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto Faith~}}, On Setsubun in {{ill|lt=2019|Q25274|WD=Q25274}} (Heisei 31), a goshuin stamp made with funds raised through crowdfunding was dedicated. As a result, goshuin stamps of Omae Shrine and Ashioyama Shrine (Bike Shrine) within the precincts were created and awarded for one month in February 2019. |title=[Kurafan] Let's make Setsubun Goshuin for Tsuina-chan and dedicate it to Omae Shrine❣️[Plan] |date=2018-10-24 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |work=Fantia [Onikko Hunter Tsuina-chan]}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/oosakijinja/entry-12414527938.html |title=Tsuina-chan goshuin dedication project |date=2018-10-26 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto faith~}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/oosakijinja/entry-12435182006.html |title=Current situation of cold red plum blossoms and Setsubun special goshuin |date=2019-01-24 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto faith~}}.: In 2019 (Reiwa 1), a story set at Omae Shrine was created in the voice drama "Onikko Hunter Tsuina-chan". ! |date=2019-07-23 |accessdate=2019-11-01 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto Faith~}}. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} ===External link=== * [https://oosakijinja.com/ Omae Shrine] (official website) * [https://ameblo.jp/oosakijinja/ Omae Shrine ~ Walking with Shinto faith ~] (Official blog) * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinjalist/oosaki.html Engishikiuchi Omae Shrine]{{Broken link|date=January 2025}} (Tochigi Prefectural Shrine Agency) * [http://www.ebisujinja.com/ Omae Ebisu Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōsaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōsaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:gōshi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:下野国の式内社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:真岡市の建築物]] [[Category:真岡市の歴史]] [[Category:栃木県の重要文化財]] [[Category:栃木県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:オートバイ]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

House of worship | Location={{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Moka City|Q498060|WD=Q498060|1=Mooka, Tochigi}}Togo 937 | Location={{Coord|36|26|57.1|N|140|01|33.2|E|scale:5000_region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Omae Shrine}} | Enshrined deity={{ill|WD=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi Okami|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Kotoshirosu|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshirosu Okami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} | Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}} | Founding={{ill|lt=Jingo Keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049|1=Jingo-keiun}} years ({{ill|lt=767 years|Q30561|WD=Q30561|1=AD 767}} - {{ill|lt=770 years|Q25021|WD=Q25021|1=770}}) | Main shrine={{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=November 9th|Q2991|WD=Q2991|1=November 9}}・{{ill|lt=November 10th|Q2988|WD=Q2988|lt=10th|1=November 10}} | Map={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Omae Shrine|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Osaki Shrine'' (Osaki Jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Moka City|Q498060|WD=Q498060|1=Mooka, Tochigi}}. {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}}. ===Enshrined God=== *God of fortune “Daikoku-sama” ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}) *His son god “Ebisu-sama” ({{ill|lt=Kotodaishu|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotodaishu Okami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}) *As the enshrined deity, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Yoyorozu no Kami|Q118906758|WD=Q118906758|1=Yaoyorozu no Kami}} It was called ``Omae-sama'' and ``Omae-daigongen'' by parishioners who worshiped it, and even today, the elders are familiar with it as ``{{ill|lt=Gongen|Q3080343|WD=Q3080343|1=Gongen}}-sama,'' and most people call it ``Omae-sama.'' ===History=== Osaki, the sacred place where Engishikinai Omae Shrine is located, is said to be a ``sacred place from the age of the gods'' in ancient oral inscriptions. The origin of the name ``Omae'' comes from the fact that ``Haganuma'' once existed in the northern area adjacent to the shrine precincts, and because it was located at the tip of the lake, it was called ``Omae (Saki) Shrine.'' The shrine building was rebuilt during {{ill|lt=Jingo Keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049|1=Jingo-keiun}} ({{ill|lt=767|Q30561|WD=Q30561|1=AD 767}} - {{ill|lt=770|Q25021|WD=Q25021|1=770}}), and has a history of over 1,500 years. During the Heian period, the branch temple of Higashieizan Kuroko Senmyo-ji Temple in Bando, Hitachi Province was enshrined as a Bettoji temple, and the head office had a {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|WD=Q854773|1=Bhaisajyaguru}} as a syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism. Also, {{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Johei|1=Jōhei}}5 years ({{ill|lt=935 years|Q24867|WD=Q24867|1=935}}) {{ill|lt=Taira no Masakado|Q1207652|WD=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado}} is {{ill|lt=Joheiten It is said that when he started the Kei no War|Q2975322|WD=Q2975322|1=Tengyō no Ran}}, he prayed for victory in the battle at Omae Shrine and performed a service at the Mitarai of {{ill|lt=Gogyo River|Q11372661|WD=Q11372661|1=Gogyō River}}. In the Middle Ages, the {{ill|lt=Haga clan|Q11615839|WD=Q11615839|1=Haga clan}} of Bando's rough warriors ``Ki-Kei Ryōtō'' built Wakairo Castle to the east of the headquarters, then {{ill|lt=Gozen Castle|Q24872500|WD=Q24872500|1=Gozen Castle}} to the south of Omae Shrine, and served as the guardian of the Omae Shrine shrine territory until the 22nd generation, Haga Juro Kiyohara Takasada.It is said that in the past, during {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Reitaisai|1=UNKNOWN}}, people chanted ``to'' and offered the head of a boar or deer, and it is said that deer meat and deer heads were offered as offerings to the gods. Even now, venison is offered as a sacred food at annual festivals. Additionally, a carp representing a three-tailed divine messenger is depicted on the large floor ceiling of the worship hall, and an image of a rising dragon is depicted on the ceiling of the worship hall. The main hall is also carved with male and female carp and Kotone Sennin's carp. In honor of the carp that is the {{ill|lt=divine messenger|Q10444973|WD=Q10444973|1=Shinshi}}, it is said that when a wish is fulfilled, a healthy carp is offered in front of the god, and the ritual is performed by pouring sacred sake over it and releasing it into the {{ill|lt=Gogyo River|Q11372661|WD=Q11372661|1=Gogyō River}}. In addition, ``Japan's No. 1 Ebisu Shrine {{ill|lt=Omae Ebisu Shrine|Q11433058|WD=Q11433058|1=Ōsaki Ebisu Shrine}}'' is enshrined on the grounds. [[File:Ashioyama Shrine.jpg|alt=Ashioyama Shrine|thumb|230x230px|Ashioyama Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bike Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}})]]{{ill|lt=2013|Q1998|WD=Q1998|1=2013}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}}25) On November 9th, the second torii gate, which had been forced to be dismantled due to the Great East Japan Earthquake, was rebuilt using cedar wood from within the prefecture. Also, in commemoration of the 62nd Jingu Shikinen Sengu, maintenance work was carried out on the {{ill|lt=Setsusha Shrine|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=Setsusha/Sususha|1=Setsumatsusha}}, which is enshrined behind the main shrine. Among them, Ashioyama Shrine, the last of its kind, is the first shrine in Japan to pray for traffic safety for {{ill|lt=motorcycles|Q34493|WD=Q34493|lt=two-wheeled vehicles|1=Motorcycle}}, and is visited by 800 to 1,000 riders a month from inside and outside the prefecture every year. [https://bike-news.jp/post/171636 2020.05.28 Bike News Editorial Department What is there at Bike Shrine? "Omae Shrine" in Tochigi Prefecture is a pilgrimage spot that also appears in the manga "Bakuon!!" Motorcycle News] {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|WD=Q25290|1=2017}} (Heisei 217) marks the 1250th anniversary of the restoration, and we are promoting commemorative projects such as relocation and renovation of the temple hall, kagura hall, shrine office, etc. From the Great Setsubun Festival on February 3 of the same year, {{ill|lt=Oiin|Q5371419|WD=Q5371419|lt=Oiin ceremony|1=Nuo folk religion}}, the three demons ({{ill|lt=Mae-oni, Go-oni|Q11397864|WD=Q11397864|1=Zenki and Goki}} In addition to {{ill|lt=Domoku|Q60995805|WD=Q60995805|1=Dōmeki}}), {{ill|lt=Oiin|Q5371419|WD=Q5371419|lt=Hosoji|1=Nuo folk religion}} was added in accordance with the ancient rituals.{{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291|1=2018}} As of December 25, 2018, Omae Shrine's main hall, shrine, and worship hall were designated as national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Properties|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}Heisei 30 December 25th, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology No. 231[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1410231.html “About the designation of national treasures and important cultural properties (buildings)”] (Agency for Cultural Affairs website, announced October 19, 2018). File:Oosaki-tenmanguu.jpg|Tenmangu Shrine ===Cultural assets=== ===Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated)=== *Honden - Built in the middle of the Edo period (1707). The building has three girder rows, two beam spaces, a hip-gable structure, one room facing the roof, and a copper plate roof. Designated on December 25, 2018. *Hideden and Worship Hall - Built in the middle of the Edo period. Hall of worship, 3 rooms in the front with girders, 5 rooms in the back, 3 rooms between the beams, gambrel style, front with staggered gables, 1 room on the front, with eaves and gables, copper plate roofing. The hall has two girder rows, one beam space, hipped structure, and copper plate roofing. Designated on December 25, 2018. ===Tochigi Prefecture designated cultural property=== *{{ill|lt=Ryobe Torii|Q11362685|WD=Q11362685|1=ryōbu torii}} *One copper lantern *Tachi Mumei Kuchichi *Paperback and ink calligraphy Heike Monogatari 11 volumes *Version of the Great Prajnaparamaya Sutra, Volume 586 *Genroku picture map *Yamauchi Meifu Merit Monument *Sangaku *Eitai Odaimikagura Monument *Omae Shrine Odai Kagura === Transportation === * Nearest station: 15 minutes walk from Moka Railway "{{ill|lt=Kita-Moka Station|Q6417569|WD=Q6417569|1=Kitamōka Station}}" * {{ill|lt=Kitakanto Expressway|Q11404370|WD=Q11404370|1=Kita-Kantō Expressway}} About 10 minutes from Moka I.C. * From JR {{ill|lt=Ishibashi Station (Tochigi Prefecture)|Q872787|WD=Q872787|lt=Ishibashi Station|1=Ishibashi Station (Tochigi)}}, take the {{ill|lt=Kanto Expressway (Tochigi Prefecture)|Q11655986|WD=Q11655986|lt=Kanto Bus|1=Kanto Transportation}} "Ishibashi Station/Moka Shako" system for about 35 minutes, get off at "Moka Shako" and walk 20 minutes. * From JR {{ill|lt=Utsunomiya Station|Q801549|WD=Q801549|1=Utsunomiya Station}}, take the Kanto bus bound for “Utsunomiya Tobu-Moka Office” for about an hour, and get off at “Omae Jinja-mae”. * Parking: Yes === Collaboration === ; Sora-chan (Sora-chan) : The main character of "Sora-chan's Journey", which is serialized in the reader submission section of the magazine "Mr.Bike BG" of {{ill|lt=Motor Magazine Company|Q11344833|WD=Q11344833|1=Motor Magazine Company}} by the manga contributor CB of Red Feathers{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/masiko-kyokusin/entry-11829355564.html |title=Sora-chan's Journey |date=2014-04-21 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Red Feather CB Blog}}. : Poster because Ashioyama Shrine, a subsidiary shrine, is called a motorcycle shrine |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto faith~}}Life-size figures{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/oosakijinja/entry-11422708297.html |title=Riders from all over the country! ! |date=2012-12-08 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto Faith~}} is located, and we are collaborating.: {{ill|lt=2013|Q1998|WD=Q1998|1=2013}} Since January 27, 2013, an event called "Sora-chan Sanshu" (only the first "Sora-chan Meeting") has been held regularly. Plenty of photos |date=2013-01-27 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto faith~}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/oosakijinja/entry-12423290082.html |title=Information about the 7th Sora-chan gathering! |date=2018-12-03 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto Faith~}}.: In addition, in conjunction with "Sora-chan Collection", illustrator Watabe☆Yu has created {{ill|lt=Honda Motors|Q9584|WD=Q9584|lt=Honda|1=Honda}}, {{ill|lt=Kawasaki Heavy Industries|Q748148|WD=Q748148|lt=Kawasaki|1=Kawasaki Heavy Industries}}, {{jalink|Suzuki} (Companies)|Q181642|Suzuki}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaha Motor|Q158888|WD=Q158888|lt=Yamaha|1=Yamaha Motor Company}} motorcycle ema are dedicated and regularly distributed{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/oosakijinja/entry-12382683852.html |title=Bike ema　Motorcycle ema from 4 domestic companies will be available soon! ! |date=2018-06-10 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto Faith~}}{{Cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190125183521/https://www.shimotsuke.co.jp/articles/-/45756 |title=Moka Omae Shrine with 4 domestic companies offering motorcycle votive tablets |date=2018-07-06 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |newspaper={{ill|lt=Shimotsu Shimbun|Q11361390|WD=Q11361390|1=Shimotsuke Shimbun}}}}. ; {{ill|lt=Tsuina-chan|Q85869165|WD=Q85869165|1=Tsuina-chan}}: Religious research related to the {{ill|lt=Japanese Oniko (character)|Q5365273|WD=Q5365273|lt=Japanese Oniko|1=Hinomoto Oniko}} project that was born on the Internet bulletin board.{{ill|lt=Moe|Q497942|WD=Q497942|1=Moe (slang)}}{{ill|lt=Character|Q95074|WD=Q95074|1=Character (arts)}}. He is said to be {{ill|lt=Oiin #Overview|Q5371419|WD=Q5371419|lt=Hosoji|1=Nuo folk religion}}, a descendant of {{ill|lt=Yakusōkaku|Q198946|WD=Q198946|1=En no Gyōja}}.: Exchanges with Omae Shrine occurred due to the appearance of the Hoso clan in the Oinashiki ceremony in 2017 (Heisei 29). |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto Faith~}}, On Setsubun in {{ill|lt=2019|Q25274|WD=Q25274|1=2019}} (Heisei 31), a goshuin stamp made with funds raised through crowdfunding was dedicated. As a result, goshuin stamps of Omae Shrine and Ashioyama Shrine (Bike Shrine) within the precincts were created and awarded for one month in February 2019. |title=[Kurafan] Let's make Setsubun Goshuin for Tsuina-chan and dedicate it to Omae Shrine❣️[Plan] |date=2018-10-24 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |work=Fantia [Onikko Hunter Tsuina-chan]}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/oosakijinja/entry-12414527938.html |title=Tsuina-chan goshuin dedication project |date=2018-10-26 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto faith~}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/oosakijinja/entry-12435182006.html |title=Current situation of cold red plum blossoms and Setsubun special goshuin |date=2019-01-24 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto faith~}}.: In 2019 (Reiwa 1), a story set at Omae Shrine was created in the voice drama "Onikko Hunter Tsuina-chan". ! |date=2019-07-23 |accessdate=2019-11-01 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto Faith~}}. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} ===External link=== * [https://oosakijinja.com/ Omae Shrine] (official website) * [https://ameblo.jp/oosakijinja/ Omae Shrine ~ Walking with Shinto faith ~] (Official blog) * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinjalist/oosaki.html Engishikiuchi Omae Shrine]{{Broken link|date=January 2025}} (Tochigi Prefectural Shrine Agency) * [http://www.ebisujinja.com/ Omae Ebisu Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōsaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōsaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:gōshi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:下野国の式内社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:真岡市の建築物]] [[Category:真岡市の歴史]] [[Category:栃木県の重要文化財]] [[Category:栃木県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:オートバイ]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

House of worship | Location={{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Moka City|Q498060|WD=Q498060|1=Mooka, Tochigi}}Togo 937 | Location={{Coord|36|26|57.1|N|140|01|33.2|E|scale:5000_region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Omae Shrine}} | Enshrined deity={{ill|WD=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi Okami|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Kotoshirosu|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshirosu Okami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} | Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}} | Founding={{ill|lt=Jingo Keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049|1=Jingo-keiun}} years ({{ill|lt=767 years|Q30561|WD=Q30561|1=AD 767}} - {{ill|lt=770 years|Q25021|WD=Q25021|1=770}}) | Main shrine={{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=November 9th|Q2991|WD=Q2991|1=November 9}}・{{ill|lt=November 10th|Q2988|WD=Q2988|lt=10th|1=November 10}} | Map={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Omae Shrine|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Osaki Shrine'' (Osaki Jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Moka City|Q498060|WD=Q498060|1=Mooka, Tochigi}}. {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}}. ===Enshrined God=== *God of fortune “Daikoku-sama” ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}) *His son god “Ebisu-sama” ({{ill|lt=Kotodaishu|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotodaishu Okami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}) *As the enshrined deity, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Yoyorozu no Kami|Q118906758|WD=Q118906758|1=Yaoyorozu no Kami}} It was called ``Omae-sama'' and ``Omae-daigongen'' by parishioners who worshiped it, and even today, the elders are familiar with it as ``{{ill|lt=Gongen|Q3080343|WD=Q3080343|1=Gongen}}-sama,'' and most people call it ``Omae-sama.'' ===History=== Osaki, the sacred place where Engishikinai Omae Shrine is located, is said to be a ``sacred place from the age of the gods'' in ancient oral inscriptions. The origin of the name ``Omae'' comes from the fact that ``Haganuma'' once existed in the northern area adjacent to the shrine precincts, and because it was located at the tip of the lake, it was called ``Omae (Saki) Shrine.'' The shrine building was rebuilt during {{ill|lt=Jingo Keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049|1=Jingo-keiun}} ({{ill|lt=767|Q30561|WD=Q30561|1=AD 767}} - {{ill|lt=770|Q25021|WD=Q25021|1=770}}), and has a history of over 1,500 years. During the Heian period, the branch temple of Higashieizan Kuroko Senmyo-ji Temple in Bando, Hitachi Province was enshrined as a Bettoji temple, and the head office had a {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|WD=Q854773|1=Bhaisajyaguru}} as a syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism. Also, {{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Johei|1=Jōhei}}5 years ({{ill|lt=935 years|Q24867|WD=Q24867|1=935}}) {{ill|lt=Taira no Masakado|Q1207652|WD=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado}} is {{ill|lt=Joheiten It is said that when he started the Kei no War|Q2975322|WD=Q2975322|1=Tengyō no Ran}}, he prayed for victory in the battle at Omae Shrine and performed a service at the Mitarai of {{ill|lt=Gogyo River|Q11372661|WD=Q11372661|1=Gogyō River}}. In the Middle Ages, the {{ill|lt=Haga clan|Q11615839|WD=Q11615839|1=Haga clan}} of Bando's rough warriors ``Ki-Kei Ryōtō'' built Wakairo Castle to the east of the headquarters, then {{ill|lt=Gozen Castle|Q24872500|WD=Q24872500|1=Gozen Castle}} to the south of Omae Shrine, and served as the guardian of the Omae Shrine shrine territory until the 22nd generation, Haga Juro Kiyohara Takasada.It is said that in the past, during {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Reitaisai|1=UNKNOWN}}, people chanted ``to'' and offered the head of a boar or deer, and it is said that deer meat and deer heads were offered as offerings to the gods. Even now, venison is offered as a sacred food at annual festivals. Additionally, a carp representing a three-tailed divine messenger is depicted on the large floor ceiling of the worship hall, and an image of a rising dragon is depicted on the ceiling of the worship hall. The main hall is also carved with male and female carp and Kotone Sennin's carp. In honor of the carp that is the {{ill|lt=divine messenger|Q10444973|WD=Q10444973|1=Shinshi}}, it is said that when a wish is fulfilled, a healthy carp is offered in front of the god, and the ritual is performed by pouring sacred sake over it and releasing it into the {{ill|lt=Gogyo River|Q11372661|WD=Q11372661|1=Gogyō River}}. In addition, ``Japan's No. 1 Ebisu Shrine {{ill|lt=Omae Ebisu Shrine|Q11433058|WD=Q11433058|1=Ōsaki Ebisu Shrine}}'' is enshrined on the grounds. [[File:Ashioyama Shrine.jpg|alt=Ashioyama Shrine|thumb|230x230px|Ashioyama Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bike Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}})]]{{ill|lt=2013|Q1998|WD=Q1998|1=2013}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}}25) On November 9th, the second torii gate, which had been forced to be dismantled due to the Great East Japan Earthquake, was rebuilt using cedar wood from within the prefecture. Also, in commemoration of the 62nd Jingu Shikinen Sengu, maintenance work was carried out on the {{ill|lt=Setsusha Shrine|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=Setsusha/Sususha|1=Setsumatsusha}}, which is enshrined behind the main shrine. Among them, Ashioyama Shrine, the last of its kind, is the first shrine in Japan to pray for traffic safety for {{ill|lt=motorcycles|Q34493|WD=Q34493|lt=two-wheeled vehicles|1=Motorcycle}}, and is visited by 800 to 1,000 riders a month from inside and outside the prefecture every year. [https://bike-news.jp/post/171636 2020.05.28 Bike News Editorial Department What is there at Bike Shrine? "Omae Shrine" in Tochigi Prefecture is a pilgrimage spot that also appears in the manga "Bakuon!!" Motorcycle News] {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|WD=Q25290|1=2017}} (Heisei 217) marks the 1250th anniversary of the restoration, and we are promoting commemorative projects such as relocation and renovation of the temple hall, kagura hall, shrine office, etc. From the Great Setsubun Festival on February 3 of the same year, {{ill|lt=Oiin|Q5371419|WD=Q5371419|lt=Oiin ceremony|1=Nuo folk religion}}, the three demons ({{ill|lt=Mae-oni, Go-oni|Q11397864|WD=Q11397864|1=Zenki and Goki}} In addition to {{ill|lt=Domoku|Q60995805|WD=Q60995805|1=Dōmeki}}), {{ill|lt=Oiin|Q5371419|WD=Q5371419|lt=Hosoji|1=Nuo folk religion}} was added in accordance with the ancient rituals.{{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291|1=2018}} As of December 25, 2018, Omae Shrine's main hall, shrine, and worship hall were designated as national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Properties|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}Heisei 30 December 25th, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology No. 231[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1410231.html “About the designation of national treasures and important cultural properties (buildings)”] (Agency for Cultural Affairs website, announced October 19, 2018). File:Oosaki-tenmanguu.jpg|Tenmangu Shrine ===Cultural assets=== ===Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated)=== *Honden - Built in the middle of the Edo period (1707). The building has three girder rows, two beam spaces, a hip-gable structure, one room facing the roof, and a copper plate roof. Designated on December 25, 2018. *Hideden and Worship Hall - Built in the middle of the Edo period. Hall of worship, 3 rooms in the front with girders, 5 rooms in the back, 3 rooms between the beams, gambrel style, front with staggered gables, 1 room on the front, with eaves and gables, copper plate roofing. The hall has two girder rows, one beam space, hipped structure, and copper plate roofing. Designated on December 25, 2018. ===Tochigi Prefecture designated cultural property=== *{{ill|lt=Ryobe Torii|Q11362685|WD=Q11362685|1=ryōbu torii}} *One copper lantern *Tachi Mumei Kuchichi *Paperback and ink calligraphy Heike Monogatari 11 volumes *Version of the Great Prajnaparamaya Sutra, Volume 586 *Genroku picture map *Yamauchi Meifu Merit Monument *Sangaku *Eitai Odaimikagura Monument *Omae Shrine Odai Kagura === Transportation === * Nearest station: 15 minutes walk from Moka Railway "{{ill|lt=Kita-Moka Station|Q6417569|WD=Q6417569|1=Kitamōka Station}}" * {{ill|lt=Kitakanto Expressway|Q11404370|WD=Q11404370|1=Kita-Kantō Expressway}} About 10 minutes from Moka I.C. * From JR {{ill|lt=Ishibashi Station (Tochigi Prefecture)|Q872787|WD=Q872787|lt=Ishibashi Station|1=Ishibashi Station (Tochigi)}}, take the {{ill|lt=Kanto Expressway (Tochigi Prefecture)|Q11655986|WD=Q11655986|lt=Kanto Bus|1=Kanto Transportation}} "Ishibashi Station/Moka Shako" system for about 35 minutes, get off at "Moka Shako" and walk 20 minutes. * From JR {{ill|lt=Utsunomiya Station|Q801549|WD=Q801549|1=Utsunomiya Station}}, take the Kanto bus bound for “Utsunomiya Tobu-Moka Office” for about an hour, and get off at “Omae Jinja-mae”. * Parking: Yes === Collaboration === ; Sora-chan (Sora-chan) : The main character of "Sora-chan's Journey", which is serialized in the reader submission section of the magazine "Mr.Bike BG" of {{ill|lt=Motor Magazine Company|Q11344833|WD=Q11344833|1=Motor Magazine Company}} by the manga contributor CB of Red Feathers{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/masiko-kyokusin/entry-11829355564.html |title=Sora-chan's Journey |date=2014-04-21 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Red Feather CB Blog}}. : Poster because Ashioyama Shrine, a subsidiary shrine, is called a motorcycle shrine |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto faith~}}Life-size figures{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/oosakijinja/entry-11422708297.html |title=Riders from all over the country! ! |date=2012-12-08 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto Faith~}} is located, and we are collaborating.: {{ill|lt=2013|Q1998|WD=Q1998|1=2013}} Since January 27, 2013, an event called "Sora-chan Sanshu" (only the first "Sora-chan Meeting") has been held regularly. Plenty of photos |date=2013-01-27 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto faith~}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/oosakijinja/entry-12423290082.html |title=Information about the 7th Sora-chan gathering! |date=2018-12-03 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto Faith~}}.: In addition, in conjunction with "Sora-chan Collection", illustrator Watabe☆Yu has created {{ill|lt=Honda Motors|Q9584|WD=Q9584|lt=Honda|1=Honda}}, {{ill|lt=Kawasaki Heavy Industries|Q748148|WD=Q748148|lt=Kawasaki|1=Kawasaki Heavy Industries}}, {{jalink|Suzuki} (Companies)|Q181642|Suzuki}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaha Motor|Q158888|WD=Q158888|lt=Yamaha|1=Yamaha Motor Company}} motorcycle ema are dedicated and regularly distributed{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/oosakijinja/entry-12382683852.html |title=Bike ema　Motorcycle ema from 4 domestic companies will be available soon! ! |date=2018-06-10 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto Faith~}}{{Cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190125183521/https://www.shimotsuke.co.jp/articles/-/45756 |title=Moka Omae Shrine with 4 domestic companies offering motorcycle votive tablets |date=2018-07-06 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |newspaper={{ill|lt=Shimotsu Shimbun|Q11361390|WD=Q11361390|1=Shimotsuke Shimbun}}}}. ; {{ill|lt=Tsuina-chan|Q85869165|WD=Q85869165|1=Tsuina-chan}}: Religious research related to the {{ill|lt=Japanese Oniko (character)|Q5365273|WD=Q5365273|lt=Japanese Oniko|1=Hinomoto Oniko}} project that was born on the Internet bulletin board.{{ill|lt=Moe|Q497942|WD=Q497942|1=Moe (slang)}}{{ill|lt=Character|Q95074|WD=Q95074|1=Character (arts)}}. He is said to be {{ill|lt=Oiin #Overview|Q5371419|WD=Q5371419|lt=Hosoji|1=Nuo folk religion}}, a descendant of {{ill|lt=Yakusōkaku|Q198946|WD=Q198946|1=En no Gyōja}}.: Exchanges with Omae Shrine occurred due to the appearance of the Hoso clan in the Oinashiki ceremony in 2017 (Heisei 29). |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto Faith~}}, On Setsubun in {{ill|lt=2019|Q25274|WD=Q25274|1=2019}} (Heisei 31), a goshuin stamp made with funds raised through crowdfunding was dedicated. As a result, goshuin stamps of Omae Shrine and Ashioyama Shrine (Bike Shrine) within the precincts were created and awarded for one month in February 2019. |title=[Kurafan] Let's make Setsubun Goshuin for Tsuina-chan and dedicate it to Omae Shrine❣️[Plan] |date=2018-10-24 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |work=Fantia [Onikko Hunter Tsuina-chan]}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/oosakijinja/entry-12414527938.html |title=Tsuina-chan goshuin dedication project |date=2018-10-26 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto faith~}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://ameblo.jp/oosakijinja/entry-12435182006.html |title=Current situation of cold red plum blossoms and Setsubun special goshuin |date=2019-01-24 |accessdate=2019-01-25 |publisher=Omae Shrine ~Walking with Shinto faith~}}.: In 2019 (Reiwa 1), a story set at Omae Shrine was created in the voice drama "Onikko Hunter Tsuina-chan". ! House of worship | Location={{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Moka City|Q498060|WD=Q498060|1=Mooka, Tochigi}}Togo 937 | Location={{Coord|36|26|57.1|N|140|01|33.2|E|scale:5000_region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Omae Shrine}} | Enshrined deity={{ill|WD=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi Okami|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Kotoshirosu|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshirosu Okami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} | Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}} | Founding={{ill|lt=Jingo Keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049|1=Jingo-keiun}} years ({{ill|lt=767 years|Q30561|WD=Q30561|1=AD 767}} - {{ill|lt=770 years|Q25021|WD=Q25021|1=770}}) | Main shrine={{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=November 9th|Q2991|WD=Q2991|1=November 9}}・{{ill|lt=November 10th|Q2988|WD=Q2988|lt=10th|1=November 10}} | Map={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Omae Shrine|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Osaki Shrine'' (Osaki Jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Moka City|Q498060|WD=Q498060|1=Mooka, Tochigi}}. {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}}. ===Enshrined God=== *God of fortune “Daikoku-sama” ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}) *His son god “Ebisu-sama” ({{ill|lt=Kotodaishu|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotodaishu Okami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}) *As the enshrined deity, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Yoyorozu no Kami|Q118906758|WD=Q118906758|1=Yaoyorozu no Kami}} It was called ``Omae-sama'' and ``Omae-daigongen'' by parishioners who worshiped it, and even today, the elders are familiar with it as ``{{ill|lt=Gongen|Q3080343|WD=Q3080343|1=Gongen}}-sama,'' and most people call it ``Omae-sama.'' ===History=== Osaki, the sacred place where Engishikinai Omae Shrine is located, is said to be a ``sacred place from the age of the gods'' in ancient oral inscriptions. The origin of the name ``Omae'' comes from the fact that ``Haganuma'' once existed in the northern area adjacent to the shrine precincts, and because it was located at the tip of the lake, it was called ``Omae (Saki) Shrine.'' The shrine building was rebuilt during {{ill|lt=Jingo Keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049|1=Jingo-keiun}} ({{ill|lt=767|Q30561|WD=Q30561|1=AD 767}} - {{ill|lt=770|Q25021|WD=Q25021|1=770}}), and has a history of over 1,500 years. During the Heian period, the branch temple of Higashieizan Kuroko Senmyo-ji Temple in Bando, Hitachi Province was enshrined as a Bettoji temple, and the head office had a {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|WD=Q854773|1=Bhaisajyaguru}} as a syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism. Also, {{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Johei|1=Jōhei}}5 years ({{ill|lt=935 years|Q24867|WD=Q24867|1=935}}) {{ill|lt=Taira no Masakado|Q1207652|WD=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado}} is {{ill|lt=Joheiten It is said that when he started the Kei no War|Q2975322|WD=Q2975322|1=Tengyō no Ran}}, he prayed for victory in the battle at Omae Shrine and performed a service at the Mitarai of {{ill|lt=Gogyo River|Q11372661|WD=Q11372661|1=Gogyō River}}. In the Middle Ages, the {{ill|lt=Haga clan|Q11615839|WD=Q11615839|1=Haga clan}} of Bando's rough warriors ``Ki-Kei Ryōtō'' built Wakairo Castle to the east of the headquarters, then {{ill|lt=Gozen Castle|Q24872500|WD=Q24872500|1=Gozen Castle}} to the south of Omae Shrine, and served as the guardian of the Omae Shrine shrine territory until the 22nd generation, Haga Juro Kiyohara Takasada.It is said that in the past, during {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Reitaisai|1=UNKNOWN}}, people chanted ``to'' and offered the head of a boar or deer, and it is said that deer meat and deer heads were offered as offerings to the gods. Even now, venison is offered as a sacred food at annual festivals. Additionally, a carp representing a three-tailed divine messenger is depicted on the large floor ceiling of the worship hall, and an image of a rising dragon is depicted on the ceiling of the worship hall. The main hall is also carved with male and female carp and Kotone Sennin's carp. In honor of the carp that is the {{ill|lt=divine messenger|Q10444973|WD=Q10444973|1=Shinshi}}, it is said that when a wish is fulfilled, a healthy carp is offered in front of the god, and the ritual is performed by pouring sacred sake over it and releasing it into the {{ill|lt=Gogyo River|Q11372661|WD=Q11372661|1=Gogyō River}}. In addition, ``Japan's No. 1 Ebisu Shrine {{ill|lt=Omae Ebisu Shrine|Q11433058|WD=Q11433058|1=Ōsaki Ebisu Shrine}}'' is enshrined on the grounds. [[File:Ashioyama Shrine.jpg|alt=Ashioyama Shrine|thumb|230x230px|Ashioyama Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bike Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}})]]{{ill|lt=2013|Q1998|WD=Q1998|1=2013}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}}25) On November 9th, the second torii gate, which had been forced to be dismantled due to the Great East Japan Earthquake, was rebuilt using cedar wood from within the prefecture. Also, in commemoration of the 62nd Jingu Shikinen Sengu, maintenance work was carried out on the {{ill|lt=Setsusha Shrine|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=Setsusha/Sususha|1=Setsumatsusha}}, which is enshrined behind the main shrine. Among them, Ashioyama Shrine, the last of its kind, is the first shrine in Japan to pray for traffic safety for {{ill|lt=motorcycles|Q34493|WD=Q34493|lt=two-wheeled vehicles|1=Motorcycle}}, and is visited by 800 to 1,000 riders a month from inside and outside the prefecture every year. [https://bike-news.jp/post/171636 2020.05.28 Bike News Editorial Department What is there at Bike Shrine? "Omae Shrine" in Tochigi Prefecture is a pilgrimage spot that also appears in the manga "Bakuon!!" Motorcycle News] {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|WD=Q25290|1=2017}} (Heisei 217) marks the 1250th anniversary of the restoration, and we are promoting commemorative projects such as relocation and renovation of the temple hall, kagura hall, shrine office, etc. From the Great Setsubun Festival on February 3 of the same year, {{ill|lt=Oiin|Q5371419|WD=Q5371419|lt=Oiin ceremony|1=Nuo folk religion}}, the three demons ({{ill|lt=Mae-oni, Go-oni|Q11397864|WD=Q11397864|1=Zenki and Goki}} In addition to {{ill|lt=Domoku|Q60995805|WD=Q60995805|1=Dōmeki}}), {{ill|lt=Oiin|Q5371419|WD=Q5371419|lt=Hosoji|1=Nuo folk religion}} was added in accordance with the ancient rituals.{{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291|1=2018}} As of December 25, 2018, Omae Shrine's main hall, shrine, and worship hall were designated as national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Properties|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}Heisei 30 December 25th, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology No. 231[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1410231.html “About the designation of national treasures and important cultural properties (buildings)”] (Agency for Cultural Affairs website, announced October 19, 2018).
